title: senna cumingii var. coquimbensis
intro: Senna cumingii var. coquimbensis, also known as the Chilean senna or Coquimbo senna, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to South America. The medicinal properties of Senna cumingii var. coquimbensis include its ability to act as a laxative due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides, which help to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. The health benefits of using this plant include the treatment of digestive issues, such as constipation and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Senna cumingii var. coquimbensis is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and can also be found in combination with other herbal ingredients in supplements designed to support digestive health.
